title: Stock Market Live, July 24: Sensex down 600 pts, Nifty near 23,700; IndiGo, Infosys drops 2%, Meesho falls 6% post Q2 results - The HinduBusinessLine
text:
Sensex Today, Nifty 50 | Stock Market Live Updates- Find here all the key highlights related to Sensex, Nifty, BSE, NSE, share prices and Indian stock markets for July 24, 2026.

Markets opened lower on Friday. At 12 noon, Sensex traded 621.82 pts or 0.81% lower at 75,769.57; and Nifty 50 declined 175.85 pts or 0.74% to 23,693.75. All sectoral indices except media and FMCG in red. Auto stocks lead decliners.

The crude oil price topped $100 a barrel sending shock waves to oil importing countries such as India. Besides, unwinding of yen carry trade will also trigger sell-off.

U.S. President Donald Trump promised a “major military punishment” for Iran and its Houthi ‌allies on Thursday after the Yemeni fighters struck two Saudi oil tankers in ⁠the Red Sea, extending the Middle East war to a second ‌major shipping chokepoint.

All 16 major sectors logged losses. The broader small-caps and mid-caps ⁠fell 0.6% each.

Follow our live coverage of Q1 results here

Meesho shares dip 6% despite narrowed Q1 loss and optimistic long-term growth guidance of 25% CAGR over five years.

Motilal Oswal Financial Services fell 8% to Rs 862.10 on the NSE, hitting a low of Rs 839.90 from the previous close of Rs 940.25.

It reported standalone PAT at Rs 664.97 crore in Q1FY27 as against Rs 532.14 crore in Q1FY26.

First Abu Dhabi Bank PJSC is offering funding up to $1.5 billion to non-resident Indians investing in their home country’s foreign-currency deposit program, according to people familiar with the matter.

Top gainers of Nifty 50: Cipla (+2.71%), HDFC Life (+0.81%), Trent (+0.78%), Reliance (+0.57%)

Top losers: Eternal (-3.59%), Bajaj Finance (-2.74%), Infosys (-2.62%)., M&M (-2.62%)

At 12 noon, Sensex traded 621.82 pts or 0.81% lower at 75,769.57; and Nifty 50 declined 175.85 pts or 0.74% to 23,693.75.

All sectoral indices except media and FMCG in red. Auto stocks lead decliners.

The board of the company approved raising up to Rs 5,000 cr via QIP/other modes

Fractal Analytics stock traded 6% lower at Rs 811.20 on the NSE, hitting a low of Rs 795 from the previous close of Rs 861.45.

It posted a 97.8 per cent jump in its consolidated profit to Rs 74.2 crore in the first quarter.

Company received communication from Senseforth AI Research Private Limited, informing that Fractal Frontiers Inc. has been dissolved with effect from July 21, 2026.

IndiGo said the Competition Commission of India (CCI) has kept the Director General’s investigation in abeyance while considering the company’s commitment application and has invited comments, objections and suggestions from stakeholders on the proposal.

Shares were down 2% to Rs 4924 on the NSE.

“The gold market showcased a highly dynamic trend this week, reflecting the impact from the tension between elevated geopolitical uncertainties and macroeconomic friction. While the surge in energy prices initially fueled the safe haven appeal, lingering inflationary concerns and the prospects of a conservative approach in the monetary policy capped the rally, kept the price in check with a modest weekly gain.

Going forward, the price trajectory of the yellow metal will largely depend on the Central Bank’s commentary and also the US Fed’s decision. It is likely that immediate rate actions may remain on pause, although, any hawkish tone regarding inflation could trigger short-term market recalibration. Having said that, support near USD 4000 remains a crucial floor for the metal.

On the demand dynamics, long term structural demand will continue to be resilient with mild jitters based on real-time economic developments, with consumer preference inclining more towards lightweight jewellery given the steep gold prices raising concerns around general affordability.”

ABDL shares were up 1% to Rs 608.45 on the NSE at 11.41 am, hitting a high of Rs 618.80 from the previous close of Rs 602.55 . During the session, the stock touched a low of Rs 580.

Its standalone PAT was up to Rs 68.19 cr in Q1FY27 as against Rs 60.91 cr in Q1 FY26.

Bank Nifty traded 397 pts lower at 56,195.40 as at 11.39 am, hitting a low of 56,023.60 from the previous close of 56,592.

All counters in red; SBI, Federal Bank, ICICI Bank, HDFC Bank and IDFC First Bank among top losers

RMC Switchgears has received Letters of Award (LOA) from Genus Power Infrastructures Limited amounting to ₹ 5 CR for UND S_Panel mounting structure 1.1KW .

Petronet LNG said it is not aware of any move by QatarEnergy to extend force majeure through mid-October 2026.

“Due to continuing hostilities in the region and blockage of Strait of Hormuz, the Force Majeure situation is persisting. It may also be noted that since commencement of Force Majeure event, Petronet LNG has been taking adequate steps to mitigate impact of Force Majeure like providing additional slots/regasification capacity.”

Shares were up 1.5% to Rs 273.65 on the NSE.

Aims to combine modern Low-Code/No-Code platforms with AI-powered automation and accelerated, AI-infused software development practices to transform business operations for the client

Sona Comstar 1QFY27 EBITDA was in line with our estimates. We expect revenue growth to accelerate, owing to (1) recovery in differential assembly business CY2026E onwards, (2) strong momentum in traction motors and railway segments and (3) incremental opportunity in differential gears. Beyond current products, we expect the Physical AI and BEV traction motor opportunity to drive medium-term growth prospects. However, valuation remains expensive at 54X one-year forward EPS, which drives SELL rating.

SRF’s 1QFY27 earnings came in sharply above estimates, but driven primarily by windfall gains in the non-chemicals segments and pricing tailwinds in refrigerants. We raise FY2027E EPS by 15% and FY2028E-29E by a more modest 7-8%, chiefly reflecting an improved outlook for the lower-multiple Performance Films segment. Valuations remain stretched in the context of a commodity-heavy business mix. Retain SELL wi
